title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a hotel manager called Nina talking about work placements in an environmentally friendly hotel. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a hotel manager called Nina talking about work placements in an environmentally friendly hotel. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: A hotel manager called Nina talks about work placements in an environmentally friendly hotel.
extracts.audio_script: Good morning. I’m Nina, and I manage GreenStay, a small hotel offering work placements to students interested in tourism and environmental practice. The hotel is in the countryside near a popular walking route. It was not originally built for visitors: the main building was once a [9]farmhouse[/9], and several original wooden features remain in the dining area. We now have eighteen guest rooms and try to reduce waste and energy use while offering a comfortable stay. Students can join us for one week during college holidays.||Placement students experience more than one department. Some expect to spend their time at reception, but there are also tasks connected with rooms, food and guest activities. Every student first attends a session about [10]customer service[/10], as being polite and able to respond helpfully is important wherever you work in the hotel. Students receive a uniform top and name badge. They should provide comfortable black shoes and bring a [11]notepad[/11], because supervisors may give instructions or useful vocabulary throughout the week. Phones should not be used while assisting guests.||Students' first morning is usually spent with our breakfast team. They do not cook hot food immediately; instead, they start by arranging the [12]breakfast buffet[/12], checking labels and ensuring that clean dishes are available. Our food policy is an important part of GreenStay. Guests often imagine all produce arrives from large suppliers, but most vegetables used in evening meals come from a nearby [13]organic farm[/13]. We also serve locally baked bread and try to choose seasonal ingredients. Any student with allergies should let the manager know before the placement begins.||Environmental work goes beyond food. In every bedroom we provide reusable bottles, and guests can refill them from a filtered water point. The hotel has recently stopped offering individual plastic shampoo containers, replacing them with wall-mounted [14]soap dispensers[/14]. Placement students may help check these while preparing rooms. Guests often ask for ideas about exploring the area. Our most popular activity is not cycling, as I had expected, but a guided [15]woodland walk[/15] led by a local nature expert each Thursday morning. Students may accompany the group if their duties allow.||Students who successfully finish the placement have an individual meeting with a supervisor and receive a written [16]placement report[/16] describing the work they completed. In the summer, we also invite former placement students to help at our [17]garden party[/17], where local producers display food and crafts for hotel guests and residents. This event raises money for improvements. Our next environmental project is not new furniture or decoration; the funds will help us install a [18]rainwater system[/18] for watering gardens and cleaning outdoor areas. Application packs include dates and travel information, and I am happy to answer questions now.

dialogue.speaker: nina
dialogue.text: Good morning. I’m Nina, and I manage GreenStay, a small hotel offering work placements to students interested in tourism and environmental practice. The hotel is in the countryside near a popular walking route. It was not originally built for visitors: the main building was once a farmhouse, and several original wooden features remain in the dining area. We now have eighteen guest rooms and try to reduce waste and energy use while offering a comfortable stay. Students can join us for one week during college holidays.||Placement students experience more than one department. Some expect to spend their time at reception, but there are also tasks connected with rooms, food and guest activities. Every student first attends a session about customer service, as being polite and able to respond helpfully is important wherever you work in the hotel. Students receive a uniform top and name badge. They should provide comfortable black shoes and bring a notepad, because supervisors may give instructions or useful vocabulary throughout the week. Phones should not be used while assisting guests.||Students' first morning is usually spent with our breakfast team. We also serve locally baked bread and try to choose seasonal ingredients. Any student with allergies should let the manager know before the placement begins.||Environmental work goes beyond food. Guests often ask for ideas about exploring the area. Our most popular activity is not cycling, as I had expected, but a guided woodland walk led by a local nature expert each Thursday morning. Students may accompany the group if their duties allow.||Students who successfully finish the placement have an individual meeting with a supervisor and receive a written placement report describing the work they completed. In the summer, we also invite former placement students to help at our garden party, where local producers display food and crafts for hotel guests and residents. Our next environmental project is not new furniture or decoration; the funds will help us install a rainwater system for watering gardens and cleaning outdoor areas. Application packs include dates and travel information, and I am happy to answer questions now.

questions.explanation: Nina says all students first attend a 'customer service' session.
